SUMMARY

This is AI generated summarization, which may have errors. For context, always refer to the full article.

GIVING BACK. Former national marathon runner Mary Joy Tabal remain active in Philippine sports, but in a different capacity.

RAPPLER

PALAWAN, Philippines – Olympian Mary Joy Tabal feels like it’s a full-circle moment.

Tabal, the first female marathon runner from the Philippines to compete in the Olympics, currently heads the delegation of the Mandaue City, Cebu team inBatang Pinoy, the national grassroots tournament where she also once competed.

“I’m glad that I am a product of Batang Pinoy, and I’ve been telling my athletes to not put too much pressure on winning,” Tabal shared.

“I just want them to get the best experience that they can have, like even if you lose, the learning they can get after competing [is important], and instilling to them that this is not the end.”

Tabal, who competed in the 2016 Rio Olympics, started as a sprinter in the 2002 edition of Batang Pinoy, representing Cebu, just a year before her first Southeast Asian (SEA) Games stint in Vietnam.

Over the years, Tabal won three SEA Games medals — a gold and two silvers — and remains the national standard in the 21K (1 hour and 16.29 minutes) and 42K (2:43.31) races.

The Cebuana also dominated local races, winning the National Milo Marathon six times from 2013 to 2018.

Now 35, Tabal said she’s enjoying her mentorship and administrative role with promising student-athletes from her home province.

“I’m very happy and excited, and I throw my full support,” said Tabal in Filipino. “Since I do a hands-on approach, I accommodate all my coaches, athletes, making sure that they are well.”

“I’m trying my best to really assist and support my entire team,” she continued, “so that they can have the best experience that they can have.”– Rappler.com

title: New role: Olympian Mary Joy Tabal turns focus on Mandaue City grassroots sports
author: Jasmine Payo
description: Mary Joy Tabal, the first Filipino female runner to compete in an Olympic marathon, hopes to lend her sporting experience as head of the Mandaue City delegation in this year’s Batang Pinoy
